title: Sticherus littoralis
intro: Sticherus littoralis, also known as the littoral fern or New Zealand fern, is a species of fern that belongs to the family Gleicheniaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties that may help to alleviate conditions such as arthritis, wounds, and infections. The bioactive constituents of Sticherus littoralis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess significant medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Sticherus littoralis is often prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, and is used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, cough, and skin irritations.
taxonomy: Sticherus littoralis belongs to the plant family Gleicheniaceae. This family is a part of the division Pteridophyta, which includes all ferns. The Gleicheniaceae family is a small and ancient group of ferns, commonly known as the "chain ferns." Sticherus littoralis is a species of fern within this family. It is native to Australia, where it grows in coastal regions.
